toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all legacy patterns and modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns identified:
1. **XML namespace and ToolsVersion** - `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and `xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003"` are unnecessary in SDK-style
2. **Manual Import of Common.props and CSharp.targets** - SDK-style handles this via `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
3. **Explicit Configuration/Platform PropertyGroups** - Debug/Release configs with default values are implicit in SDK-style


4. **ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects** - These are either obsolete or have sensible defaults in SDK-style
5. **Deterministic setting** - Already true by default
6. **System assembly references** - Automatically included when targeting .NET Framework via SDK-style
7. **Explicit Compile includes** - SDK-style uses automatic globbing, so all .cs files are included without needing to list them
8. **AssemblyInfo.cs** - SDK-style generates assembly attributes automatically, with metadata specified directly in the .csproj
9. **TargetFrameworkVersion** - I'll convert this to the modern `TargetFramework` property using the shorthand `net472`

Now I'm going to modernize the project file and walk through what's changing.

  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
toolArgs.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolArgs.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.content:
Done. Here's a summary of the **legacy patterns removed** and how they're simplified:

| Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Equivalent |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion`, XML namespace | Replaced by `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` attribute |
| Manual `Import` of Common.props/CSharp.targets | Implicit via SDK |
| `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| `OutputType Library` | Default for SDK projects |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | `TargetFramework net472` |
| `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK defaults |
| Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | SDK provides sensible defaults |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="..."/>` items | SDK globs all `*.cs` automatically |
| `<Reference Include="System.*"/>` | Implicit with framework targeting pack |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (auto-generated) |

The project went from **47 lines → 12 lines**, with no loss of functionality.
